name: Steak Kew (Canton)
recipeIngredient: 1 lb Flank, skirt steak or other tender beef, cut into 1-inch squares
1 lb Chinese or regular broccoli, or fresh asparagus tips, or
1 lb Firm ripe tomatoes, cut into wedges
recipeInstructions:
1. Combine beef, onion, hoisin sauce, sesame oil, soy, pepper, cornstarch, garlic, and ginger. Let stand for 15 minutes.
2. Blanch snow peas, broccoli, asparagus, or tomatoes for 1 to 2 minutes; drain and place on heated platter; keep warm if using tomatoes.
3. Heat peanut oil in a wok or skillet and stir-fry meat and onion mixture for 1 to 2 minutes; meat should be well seared but still pink inside.
4. Immediately pour over blanched vegetable and serve. Alternatively, place on heated platter and surround with tomato wedges.
Variation with oyster sauce: Omit hoisin sauce and sesame oil and add one tablespoon oyster sauce.
Variation with hot bean paste: Omit hoisin sauce and add 2 teaspoons hot bean paste.
